data.Modification: Iodoacetamide derivative/Addition of Glycine/Addition of G

project.title: Cyclin dependent kinase 5 (CDK5) regulates the circadian clock
project.projectDescription: We show that the cyclin-dependent kinase 5 (CDK5) regulates the mammalian circadian clock via phosphorylation of PER2. CDK5 phosphorylated PER2 at serine residue 394 (S394) as shown by an in vitro kinase assay.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Filter-aided in vitro kinase assays and mass spectrometry analyses were performed essentially as described (doi.org/10.1016/j.molcel.2018.10.040). Briefly, recombinant Cdk5/p35 (Millipore) was incubated with the GST-fused PER2 protein fragment. On 10 kDa MW-cutoff filters (PALL) samples were incubated in kinase buffer containing 50 mM HEPES, pH 7.4, 150 mM NaCl, 0.625 mM DTT, Phostop tablets (Roche), 6.25 mM MgCl2, and 1.8 mM ATP (at 30°C for 1 h). Samples without ATP were used as negative control. Assays were quenched by 8 M urea and 1 mM DTT. Protein digestion for MS analysis was performed overnight (PMID: 19377485). Phosphopeptides were enriched by metal oxide affinity enrichment using titanium dioxide (GL Sciences Inc., Tokyo, Japan) (PMID: 26633130).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The MS raw files were analysed using MaxQuant Software version 1.4.1.2 (PMID: 19029910) for peak detection, quantification and peptide identification using a full length Uniprot Mouse database (April, 2016) and common contaminants such as keratins and enzymes used for digestion as reference.

references.referenceLine: Brenna A, Olejniczak I, Chavan R, Ripperger JA, Langmesser S, Cameroni E, Hu Z, De Virgilio C, Dengjel J, Albrecht U. Cyclin dependent kinase 5 (CDK5) regulates the circadian clock. Elife. 2019 8

Comment[Instrument]: QExactive Plus mass spectrometer coupled to an EasyLC 1000 nanoflow-HPLC
Comment[GradientTime]: separation ramp from 5–30% B within 85 min
Comment[IonizationType]: NanoESI spray voltage was set to 2.3 kV
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: Mass spectrometers were operated in the data-dependent mode
Comment[CollisionEnergy]: using a normalized collision energy of 25%
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: The MS/MS tolerance was set to 20 ppm
